basics.name: Alexander DiCaprio
basics.label: Data and AI Strategist
basics.summary: Experienced Strategist with 20 years of experience in providing innovative leadership in the design, development, communication, and implementation of data and AI-powered solutions that democratizes data enabling data-driven business decisions and operational efficiencies.

work.name: U.S. Department of State - Center for Analytics
work.summary: Portfolio Manager transforming how the Department leverages Artificial Intelligence to enhance data-driven diplomacy, streamline workflows, and build AI capacity.
highlights: Deployed a governed cloud based data science and AI sandbox environment in Azure Databricks, serving over 1,000 Department of State data scientists worldwide.

Managed portfolio of product teams, consisting of over twenty data scientists, analysts, engineers, and full stack developers; building platforms, infrastructure as code, and generative AI experiments.
Architect of the Department of State's AI Marketplace, enabling users to identify, qualify, and deploy trusted AI platforms.
Coordinated stakeholder engagement and communications domestically and abroad; building consensus on the Departments AI roadmap strategy.

Architected the Department first Azure Kubernetes deployment; enabling data scientists to request GPU-powered development environments, that could securely and reliably train data models to accelerate AI deployments.

work.name: ADiCaprio Management Consulting, LLC
work.summary: Delivered human-centered data solutions to drive business decisions, improve operational efficiencies, and enhance overall performance.
highlights: Developed Data Management and Data Governance frameworks, data strategies, and wireframes to support the development of Monitoring and Evaluation databases.
Automated business processes for client though Power Platform development realizing over $100K in cost savings.

work.name: MetaPhase Consulting
work.summary: Director of federal contracts, focusing on leading data-driven development initiatives across the federal government.
highlights: Directed multiple distributed teams of data scientists across Federal agencies, fostering company culture through mentorship, professional development, and leadership,
Developed foundational strategic Data Management, Data and AI Literacy, and Governance frameworks enable Federal clients to succeed in implementing their data driven goals.
Captured over $20M in Federal contracts through business development, client relationships, and strategic engagements.

work.name: Booz Allen Hamilton
work.summary: Technical Project Manager leading complex IT data analytics projects and programs across multiple Federal agencies and cross-functional teams.
highlights: Leveraged both Agile and traditional Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) frameworks for project execution.
Managed data driven initiatives that automated processes and streamline workflows for increased efficiency.
Developed and implemented data models, BI dashboards, and reporting solutions to enable data-driven insights.

work.name: Boeing
work.summary: Applied data analytics and Earned Value Management (EVM) techniques to manage material costs, integrate schedules, and maintain performance measurement baselines.
highlights: Applied EVM techniques to establish cost and schedule baselines, analyze data discrepancies, and resolve critical path conflicts.

Engineered an automated data pipeline into a relational database to reconcile transactional data for a complex V-22 aircraft block. This transformation compressed the analysis timeline by over 90%, enabling near real-time decision-making and significantly reducing manual overhead for global logistics operations.
Led a cross-functional team to automate a manual approval processes, achieving over $100K in documented cost savings through digital automation.

projects.name: Funhouse
projects.description: Architected Funhouse, an enterprise wide Azure Databricks AI sandbox enabling 1,000+ data scientists to securely leverage open-source Python/R and Azure AI services. This IDE accelerated the delivery of hundreds of AI use cases, modernizing operations across the Department of State’s domestic and international posts.

projects.name: Prompter API
projects.description: Managed the deployment of the Department of State’s first enterprise AI gateway, 'Prompter.' Prompter was engineered as a C# wrapper around Azure OpenAI that democratized LLM access for departmental developers while implementing granular governance for cost attribution and usage telemetry.

projects.name: AI Marketplace
projects.description: Defined the strategic vision for the Department of State’s AI Marketplace, a human-centered ecosystem construct designed to connect a globally diverse population of users with vetted, trusted AI platforms. Specifically, developing a AI Maturity Pillar model based on 100+ hours of stakeholder discovery, establishing enterprise standards for ethical AI, bias mitigation, and risk-aware deployment.

projects.name: Human Resources Automation
projects.description: Engineered the automation of HR operations at MetaPhase through low-code Power Platform solutions.  Optimizing and automating award approval flows, training and compliance tracking, and transactional HR Audit Logs system to ensure data compliance and operational transparency.

projects.name: Data Literacy Framework
projects.description: Architected the USCIS Data Literacy Framework and maturity model that defined the strategic goals for building a Data and AI informed USCIS.  Specifically, by identifying key skills, competencies, and learning pathways for personas to enhance their capacity over time.

projects.name: Data Strategy
projects.description: Advisory to the Chief Data Officer to manage the deployment the initial USCIS Data Strategy. Specifically through the development of a comprehensive review of the organizational structures, identification of vision, goals, and objectives, alignment to Executive Orders and Congressional Mandates, and office skill assessments.

projects.name: Machine Learning and Data Analytics
projects.description: Orchestrated the migration of a legacy, excel-based, enterprise wide performance reporting tool; modernizing an archaic solution into a high-performance Python and SQL-based ETL pipeline within Databricks. By automating data wrangling, templatizing data inputs, and integrating Tableau for real-time visualization, our team compressed the production timeline from 30 days to 72 hours, significantly enhancing data transparency and stakeholder trust.

education.institution: Case Western Reserve University - Weatherhead School of Management
courses: MIDS 470 - Analyzing Mobility and Mobile e-business

awards.title: Meritorious Honors
awards.summary: For leading a democratized data and AI development environment for 1,000+ data scientists, providing secure, enterprise-wide access to advanced Python/R libraries and AI endpoints.

awards.title: Superior Honors
awards.awarder: Department of State - Assistant Secretary Phee
awards.summary: Recognized for excellence and innovation in developing datavis technologies that institutionalized data-driven diplomacy, enhanced efficiency, and secured over $750,000 in long-term savings.
